summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a fun, upbeat ’89 party show featuring rare “Race Is On” and the beautiful “Black Muddy River.”
review: Both reviewers praise this recording as an excellent sound‑board source that lets the lively, quirky setlist shine. The show is described as a “party show” with upbeat choices – Good Times, Pocky Way, Samson – and special highlights like the rare Race Is On and the gorgeous Black Muddy River, even if Jerry sounds a bit tired on the latter. The only criticism is a nit‑pick about unnecessary track splits, not the music or audio itself.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is consistently praised (upbeat set, standout rare tracks) while recording quality is called “excellent” by one reviewer and unremarked on by the other; weighted average yields ~4.3 stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers note an excellent sound‑board copy with crisp, clear audio; no sonic flaws mentioned. The only technical gripe concerns the editor’s decision to split a continuous jam into separate tracks.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A quirky, upbeat set that ends the first set unusually with “China Rider” and launches a second set with high‑energy numbers like Good Times, Pocky Way, and Samson, giving the concert a party atmosphere.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Sounds a little tired on Black Muddy River, which oddly adds to the song’s feel.
